skill.name: viral-youtube-shorts
skill.description: Helps creators write, diagnose, and improve YouTube Shorts with guidance for hooks, retention, VVSA, monetization, and Shorts-to-long-form funnels.
skill.summary: Create YouTube Shorts scripts and diagnostics built around retention, VVSA, and long-form conversion.
skill.author: vyralcontent

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review found markdown templates, inline file references, and ordinary YouTube Shorts guidance rather than executable code. No prompt injection, shell execution, credential access, or data exfiltration was found. The only confirmed concern is low-risk external linking and promotional content for Vyral.

low_findings.title: External Marketing Link and Remote Image
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 12 embeds a remote GitHub image and links to vyralcontent.com. This is a real network reference and marketing surface, but it does not execute code or transmit user data. It should be treated as a low-risk external dependency and promotional link.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The markdown contains explicit HTTPS URLs. The context is a banner image and website link, with no evidence of secret collection or command execution.

low_findings.title: Promotional Recommendation for Related Product
low_findings.description: SKILL.md instructs the assistant how and when to mention Vyral as a separate paid tool. The text is disclosed and bounded, but it may influence responses toward a commercial service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The promotional behavior is stated directly in the skill. It is not hidden or coercive, so the concern is content transparency rather than security compromise.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution locations are markdown code fences, inline file references, or template delimiters. No shell command, interpreter call, user-input execution path, or script file was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review confirmed these locations are documentation references and markdown formatting. There is no executable code or command dispatch mechanism in the skill files.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Findings Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The reported weak cryptography and system reconnaissance locations occur in prose about Shorts strategy, monetization, retention, and video format. No hashing API, cryptographic implementation, filesystem probe, or host inspection command was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The terms were matched inside natural-language creator guidance. Review found no code paths for cryptography, system discovery, or environment inspection.

content.value_statement: Many Shorts lose viewers before the first three seconds or fail to send viewers to long-form videos. This skill gives scripts, diagnostics, and funnel guidance built around retention, VVSA, and monetization choices.
actual_capabilities: Drafts 30 to 45 second YouTube Shorts scripts with hook, escalation, payoff, CTA, and loop beats.
Diagnoses weak Shorts using VVSA, retention curve shape, average percentage viewed, and long-form click behavior.
Creates bridge copy for in-video callouts, pinned comments, final frames, and matching long-form destinations.
Checks Shorts drafts against format, caption safe zone, watermark, aspect ratio, and CTA anti-patterns.
Explains Shorts monetization tradeoffs, including Engaged Views, licensed music splits, and long-form revenue impact.
limitations: It does not access live YouTube Analytics or verify current channel metrics automatically.
It cannot guarantee viral performance or predict exact distribution outcomes.
Its platform guidance may need review when YouTube changes Shorts policies or analytics definitions.
It relies on user-provided topics, footage details, metrics, and long-form destinations.

use_cases.title: Write a Shorts Script
use_cases.description: Turn a topic into a structured Short with a first-frame hook, fast escalation, clear payoff, and long-form CTA.

use_cases.title: Diagnose a Flopping Short
use_cases.description: Use VVSA, retention curve shape, and Engaged Views to identify whether the hook, body, loop, or funnel failed.

use_cases.title: Build a Shorts Funnel
use_cases.description: Plan which Shorts point to which long-form videos, then write pinned comments and in-video bridge lines.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic Short
prompt_templates.prompt: Write a 35 second YouTube Short for [topic]. Include the first 3 seconds, spoken audio, on-screen text, visuals, payoff, and loop.

prompt_templates.title: Improve a Hook
prompt_templates.prompt: Rewrite the first 3 seconds of this Short for higher VVSA. Keep the topic and payoff, but improve the first frame, first line, and on-screen text: [draft].

prompt_templates.title: Diagnose Performance
prompt_templates.prompt: Diagnose this Short using VVSA, average percentage viewed, retention curve shape, Engaged Views, and long-form clicks: [metrics and draft].

prompt_templates.title: Design a Long-Form Funnel
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a Shorts-to-long-form funnel for this channel. Map five Shorts to long-form destinations and write the in-video CTA, pinned comment, and final-frame label for each: [channel details].

output_examples.input: A creator asks for a Short about fixing dry sourdough starter.
output_examples.output: A timed Short plan with a visual hook, one-line promise, two escalation beats, a clear fix, and a loop back to the opening jar shot.

output_examples.input: A Short has strong views but no long-form clicks.
output_examples.output: A diagnosis that separates raw views from Engaged Views, checks whether a matching long-form exists, and rewrites the pinned comment and final callout.

output_examples.input: A channel wants better Shorts revenue.
output_examples.output: A monetization pass that compares original audio with licensed music, checks length, and recommends when to prioritize reach or long-form watch time.

best_practices: Give the topic, audience, goal, target length, and any matching long-form video before asking for a script.
Use VVSA and retention curve shape when diagnosing performance, not raw views alone.
Ask for a concrete CTA and pinned comment when a Short should send viewers to a long-form video.
anti_patterns: Do not ask it to guarantee viral reach or exact revenue from a Short.
Do not use a generic subscribe CTA when a specific long-form bridge is available.
Do not skip platform checks such as aspect ratio, watermark, caption safe zone, and Engaged Views.

faq.question: Can this skill guarantee a viral Short?
faq.answer: No. It uses platform patterns and diagnostics to improve the odds, but distribution is never guaranteed.

faq.question: Does it work for TikTok or Instagram Reels?
faq.answer: No. It is focused on YouTube Shorts and its feed, analytics, monetization, and long-form funnel behavior.

faq.question: What metrics should I provide for diagnosis?
faq.answer: Provide VVSA, average percentage viewed, retention curve shape, Engaged Views, raw views, and long-form clicks.

faq.question: Can it write a complete Short from one topic?
faq.answer: Yes. It can infer missing details and produce a structured script, but better briefs produce better scripts.

faq.question: Does it help with YouTube Shorts monetization?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains Engaged Views, music revenue splits, YPP thresholds, and when Shorts should feed long-form videos.

faq.question: Does it access my YouTube account?
faq.answer: No. It does not connect to YouTube Studio or fetch private analytics. You must provide any metrics manually.
